content: Imagine sending a message online as if you’re mailing a letter. Without protection, anyone along the way could open and read it. Encryption works like a special lock for your message, turning it into a secret code that only the intended recipient can unlock and understand. When you send a message, encryption scrambles the information so it looks like gibberish to anyone trying to intercept it. Only the person with the right “key” can unscramble and read it. This process happens instantly and automatically, keeping your communication private and safe from hackers or eavesdroppers. Thanks to encryption, your sensitive information—whether it’s a business email or a personal note—remains confidential, giving you peace of mind that your messages are secure in today’s digital world.
